topic: Schumann suppression G3 storm — c6 re-attack after decider self-applied c5 patches (H1 TLDR rewrite, H2 ISS-1418 timestamp-forward, H3 SPE/CME mechanism, H4 cross-doc W027-PRED-R006, H5 plane-parallel axial-mode wording)
change_summary: Decider self-applied all five c5 patches between c5 and c6: (1) tldr_evidence rewritten 446→557 chars to preserve Pazos-primary / S&F-supporting-SPE structure (closes c5 H1); (2) PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 registration-date sentences added to V51.1 Update paragraph (closes c5 H2 / ISS-1418); (3) SPE/CME mechanism parenthetical rewritten with correct direction — SPE protons (10–100 MeV) penetrate deeper into D-region, CME-driven auroral electrons (1–10 keV) deposit primarily in E-region/thermosphere with secondary D-region coupling (closes c5 H3); (4) predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning updated to match WIN-061's split-direction framing rather than asserting standard physics uniformly predicts suppression (closes c5 H4); (5) 'lowest possible frequency' rewritten to 'lowest plane-parallel axial-mode frequency from this H(r), ~8.8 Hz — a first-order lower bound' (closes c5 H5). detail_evidence length 5639 → 6385 chars (+746).
our_argument_summary: All five c5 holes (H1 TLDR conflation, H2 ISS-1418 timestamp-forward, H3 SPE/CME mechanism direction, H4 cross-doc W027-PRED-R006, H5 'lowest possible' heuristic) are resolved in the c6 text. The Self-Contradicted verdict stands on three converging bases (~22 Hz baseline overshoot, WIN-038 pole-only floor, dome's own Unscored-vs-observed contradiction) with each base now individually defensible. The reader-facing summary layer (claim, finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_verdict_text) is internally consistent and aligned with the detail. The PRED-SR-SUPPRESS / PRED-093 timestamp arithmetic now appears in the V51.1 Update paragraph, closing the highest-leverage open attack. The SPE/CME mechanistic parenthetical now describes the correct direction. The cross-document inconsistency with W027-PRED-R006 is resolved by mirroring the split-direction framing in predictions.json. A sympathetic dome defender reading the c6 text has substantially less to work with than at c5. Residual attacks: (1) NEW — predictions.json structured fields for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 contradict the same records' detail_reasoning prose: both have data_predates_registration:false and is_genuinely_prospective:true while the prose argues the opposite (data was available before registration; prospectivity is hollow). A defender running a structured query over our predictions.json would conclude the predictions are clean, contradicting the prose argument we now cite from WIN-061. (2) Carry-forward unrealized-strengthening — Timestamp Error tab cross-reference (c5 stronger argument 1) still not wired; the new 'cryptographically-timestamped duplicate' sentence is the perfect anchor point and remains a one-sentence improvement. (3) Carry-forward — WIN-038 pole-only floor cross-reference still not wired in WIN-061 paragraph 1. (4) Carry-forward — WIN-034 copper firmament mutual-exclusion still not wired in V51.1 Update paragraph. The verdict confidence rises from c5 (0.94) to c6 (0.96) because every direct critique on the WIN-061 prose itself is now addressed; remaining work is a separate predictions.json metadata patch plus three optional cross-reference strengthenings. None of the residuals threaten the verdict.

holes_found.description: NEW — predictions.json structured fields for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 contradict the same records' detail_reasoning prose. Both records have data_predates_registration:false and is_genuinely_prospective:true and our_verdict:pending. But PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning explicitly says: 'Schumann resonance monitoring data is available in near-real-time, meaning the author had access to the Schumann amplitude response during the March 20-22 G3 event before registering this prediction' and 'the prospective credibility is substantially weakened by the combination of suspicious timing, absent model derivation, and a monitor that appears blind to the very events it should be tracking.' PRED-093 detail_reasoning says: 'observe the effect, then register the prediction, then claim it is prospective because the NEXT instance has not yet occurred.' Both prose arguments contradict data_predates_registration:false (the data — SR amplitude during the March 20-22 event — DID exist before the March 23 / April 4 registrations) and is_genuinely_prospective:true (the prose argues prospectivity is hollow). WIN-061's V51.1 Update paragraph now cites these timestamp dates and the post-hoc argument; a defender running a structured query against our predictions.json would see is_genuinely_prospective:true and data_predates_registration:false and conclude these are clean prospective predictions, contradicting the WIN-061 narrative we just strengthened. This is a within-record AND cross-document inconsistency. On the other side: a sympathetic reading is that 'data' in data_predates_registration refers to the test data (a FUTURE G3 storm SR response, which has not occurred), not the contextual data (the March 2026 storm, which had occurred and was available). Under that reading, the field is technically true. But that reading accepts the dome's framing — 'next G3' is unknown so the prediction is technically prospective — which is exactly what the prose argues against. Either we redefine the field semantics to mean 'was relevant background data available before registration' (then both fields should be true), or we add a separate field 'background_data_available_before_registration' to capture the post-hoc concern without ambiguity. As-is, our structured metadata silently agrees with the dome while our prose argues against it.
holes_found.recommendation: File an issue against predictions.json metadata semantics. Two options: (A) Update PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 to data_predates_registration:true and is_genuinely_prospective:false, on the reading that the relevant SR-storm-response data was available before registration even though the test event has not yet recurred. Add a clarifying note in the schema documentation about what 'data' means. (B) Add a new field 'background_data_available_before_registration' to capture the post-hoc concern separately, leaving the existing fields as-is for the formal-prospectivity status. Option A is simpler and aligns with the WIN-061 prose argument; option B preserves both readings. Either way, the current state — prose contradicts structured fields within the same record — is exploitable. Cross-reference: this is a generalization of c5 H4 (W027-PRED-R006 cross-doc framing); H4 fixed the inter-document inconsistency, this fixes the intra-record inconsistency.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward of c5 stronger_arguments item 1: Timestamp Error tab cross-reference still not wired in WIN-061 V51.1 Update paragraph. The c6 text now says 'A prediction and its cryptographically-timestamped duplicate, both registered after the only triggering event, cannot establish prospective status no matter the hash.' This sentence is the perfect anchor for a one-clause cross-reference like '(see Timestamp Error tab for the general wrong-side-timestamping pattern PRED-093 instantiates).' This would create a two-way citation loop: WIN-061 surfaces the canary-pattern in a second location, and the Timestamp Error tab gains a concrete dome-side example. The omission is not a verdict-threatening hole — it is an unrealized strengthening — but the leverage is high (low-cost insertion, two-way reinforcement) and the c5 review explicitly recommended it. On the other side: the PRED-093 sentence as-written is already self-contained and doesn't require the cross-reference to land its punch. Some readers prefer text without cross-tab navigation churn. Severity stays minor for that reason.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider self-apply or short-string EXP: append a parenthetical cross-reference to the Timestamp Error tab on the existing 'cryptographically-timestamped duplicate' sentence. Suggested wording above. One sentence, no other changes.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward of c5 stronger_arguments item 2: WIN-038 pole-only floor cross-reference still not wired in WIN-061 paragraph 1. WIN-038 (Self-Contradicted) independently establishes that the pole-only cavity floor H(0) gives f = 8.78 Hz, above 7.83 Hz at every radius. WIN-061 paragraph 1 now contains the H_eff disclosure with its own pole-only ~8.8 Hz number, but does not cross-reference WIN-038 as a second-line independent basis. A single sentence — 'Independently, WIN-038 documents that the pole-only cavity floor H(0)=8,537 km gives f = 8.78 Hz, above 7.83 Hz at every radius — a second Self-Contradicted basis that does not depend on the averaging choice.' — would be a free strengthening. The argument exists in the c6 text indirectly (the H_eff disclosure) but is not wired to WIN-038's independent verdict. Carry-forward severity minor; this has been open since c4.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider self-apply or short-string EXP: insert one sentence at the end of paragraph 1 (after 'no averaging choice can reach the observed baseline)') cross-referencing WIN-038's independent pole-only basis. Suggested wording above.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward of c5 stronger_arguments item 3: WIN-034 copper firmament mutual-exclusion still not wired in WIN-061 V51.1 Update paragraph. WIN-034 (Unfalsifiable) describes the dome's claim of cast copper/bronze firmament. Copper electrical conductivity (~6e7 S/m) is invariant under G3-storm particle precipitation energies. If the firmament is copper, no boundary-side storm-time conductivity change is available as a mechanism for the claimed suppression — any storm-time SR response must be ionospheric (i.e., standard physics), and per paragraph 1 the standard response is mixed-direction. The dome therefore must either abandon the copper firmament to preserve a dome-specific storm mechanism, or keep copper and cede the storm mechanism to standard ionospheric physics (which predicts mixed direction, not suppression). One closing sentence in the V51.1 Update paragraph would close the mutual-exclusion loop and surface WIN-034's relevance. Carry-forward severity minor; has been open since c4.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider self-apply or short-string EXP: append one sentence to the V51.1 Update paragraph: 'The dome's copper-firmament claim (WIN-034) compounds the difficulty: copper conductivity is invariant under G3-storm particle precipitation energies, so the boundary-side cavity response cannot supply a dome-specific suppression mechanism — any storm-time SR effect must be ionospheric, and per paragraph 1 the standard ionospheric response is mixed-direction.'

stronger_arguments.description: Predictive-test framing: the dome's monitoring infrastructure failure to score the March 20-22 G3 event is a stronger argument than currently surfaced. The dome built a falsification criterion ('G3+ storm DOES NOT cause >30% amplitude drop within 6 hours') and a Domain 9 dashboard to score it. The criterion never fired despite a confirmed G3 event. Three possibilities: (a) the Kp-detection code has a bug, (b) the SR-amplitude fetch failed silently, (c) the author chose not to score the event. Each undermines the dome's automation narrative. Currently WIN-061 paragraph 1 mentions the Unscored status as a self-contradiction with the V51.1 Update paragraph's 35% suppression narrative. The stronger version would name the three explanations and note that all three fail the dome's claim of fully automated, no-cherry-picking verification — making the monitoring failure itself a Self-Contradiction basis (separate from the ~22 Hz baseline). A deeper dive into monitor.py source (raw-text/monitor.py) to determine which of the three explanations is actually the case would convert this from a possible-Self-Contradiction to a verifiable one.
sources: raw-text/monitor.py (Kp detection logic, SR amplitude fetch path)
data/predictions.json PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning (mentions the three-possibilities argument briefly)

stronger_arguments.description: ISS-1418 closure quality check: the new V51.1 Update sentences cite predictions.json registration dates (2026-03-23 and 2026-04-04) and the Kp=7 March 20-22 storm. The closure is functionally correct. A higher-leverage strengthening would add NOAA SWPC's specific G3 confirmation URL or DOI as a cite-able source, so a reader can verify the storm dates without trusting our prose. SWPC publishes G3 alerts at swpc.noaa.gov/news; a permalink for the March 20-22 event would convert 'NOAA SWPC confirmed' from an assertion to a verifiable claim. This is a low-cost reinforcement of an already-strong addition.
sources: data/predictions.json PRED-SR-SUPPRESS detail_reasoning (asserts NOAA SWPC confirmation)

stronger_arguments.description: Schumann formula scrutiny: the c6 text adopts c/(4H_eff) as the cavity-mode formula and derives ~22 Hz from H_eff ~3,400 km. This is a plane-parallel waveguide formula. Real Schumann resonances are spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity modes given by f_n = (c/2*pi*a)*sqrt(n*(n+1)) where a is Earth's radius (~10.6 Hz theoretical for n=1, ~7.83 Hz observed with losses). The dome's flat disc + curved firmament geometry is NOT physically equivalent to either the spherical Earth cavity OR a uniform plane-parallel waveguide. By adopting c/(4H_eff), our analysis implicitly accepts the dome's formula choice. A stronger argument would: (a) show that EVEN ON THE DOME'S OWN TERMS the prediction fails (current approach), AND (b) note that the dome's formula choice is itself non-standard — the spherical Schumann mode does not become a quarter-wave plane-parallel mode in any limit of the dome geometry. This converts the argument from 'wrong number from the right formula' to 'wrong number from the wrong formula too.' Marginal value because the current refutation is already strong, but worth flagging if the dome ever responds with 'we used the right formula and got the wrong number because of measurement error.'
sources: Standard Schumann resonance formula (Sentman 1995 review; Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 already cited)
Dome's H(r) formulation — needs source citation in raw-text/ if available

kernel_of_truth.description: Storm-time Schumann perturbations are real and well-studied. The dome correctly identifies the phenomenon class. The V51.1 falsification acknowledgement of the original W027 elevation prediction demonstrates genuine epistemic discipline (now credited explicitly in the V51.1 Update paragraph). The decider's c5-to-c6 patch batch is a clean, complete close on every c5 hole — every recommendation lands as suggested.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: (1) No dome equation selects suppression over enhancement a priori. (2) Magnitude (30%) and time window (6 hours) are not derived from dome geometry. (3) ~22 Hz baseline overshoot persists; no averaging choice of H(r) reaches the observed 7.83 Hz. (4) Replacement prediction family (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS, PRED-093) was registered after the only triggering event; the c6 text now cites the timestamp arithmetic explicitly. (5) Dome monitor still shows 'Unscored' for the very event the dome narrative cites. None of these are dissolved by the c6 patch batch — the c6 patches improve the EXPRESSION of the verdict, not the verdict itself.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender reading the c6 WIN-061 would construct this defense: 'Every direct critique on the WIN-061 prose itself from the c5 review has been addressed. The TLDR is now Pazos-primary / S&F-supporting-SPE; the V51.1 Update paragraph cites PRED-SR-SUPPRESS (March 23) and PRED-093 (April 4) registration dates plus the cryptographic-hash content-integrity vs temporal-priority distinction; the SPE/CME mechanistic parenthetical now correctly has SPE protons going deeper into the D-region and CME-driven auroral electrons depositing primarily in the E-region/thermosphere; the lowest-possible-frequency claim is now the lowest plane-parallel axial-mode frequency with first-order lower-bound disclosure; W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning in predictions.json is now consistent with WIN-061 paragraph 2's split-direction framing. Three converging Self-Contradicted bases (~22 Hz baseline overshoot, WIN-038 pole-only floor, dome''s own Unscored-vs-observed contradiction) each individually carry the verdict. Three attacks survive: (A) Your own predictions.json metadata fields (data_predates_registration:false, is_genuinely_prospective:true) for both PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 contradict the prose in the same records, which argue these predictions are post-hoc. A structured-query reader sees the fields and concludes the predictions are clean — exactly what your prose denies. This is a within-record inconsistency, more exploitable than the c5 cross-doc one because both readings are inside the same JSON record. (B) You added the cryptographically-timestamped-duplicate sentence and named the canary pattern in plain English, but did not cross-reference the Timestamp Error tab. The two-way citation loop is one parenthetical away. (C) You have the H_eff disclosure with pole-only ~8.8 Hz, but you did not wire WIN-038's independent pole-only floor as a second basis. Cross-reference is one sentence; cumulative effect is large. (D) You did not wire WIN-034's copper firmament as a mutual-exclusion mechanism for the V51.1 Update paragraph. One sentence; cumulative effect is large.' These are all polish-level critiques. None threaten the Self-Contradicted verdict.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Priority order for next EXPs or decider self-applies: (1) MODERATE — File issue against predictions.json metadata semantics for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 (data_predates_registration:false vs prose). Either update field semantics or add a clarifying companion field. This is the only c6 finding above minor severity. (2) MINOR — Decider self-apply: append one parenthetical to the cryptographically-timestamped-duplicate sentence cross-referencing the Timestamp Error tab. (3) MINOR — Decider self-apply: append one sentence to paragraph 1 cross-referencing WIN-038's pole-only floor. (4) MINOR — Decider self-apply: append one sentence to V51.1 Update paragraph cross-referencing WIN-034's copper firmament mutual-exclusion. (5) MINOR — Spelling/style polish ('behaviour' -> 'behavior'). All five are independent and can be applied in a single decider run if budget allows. Verdict confidence c5 0.94 -> c6 0.96 because every direct prose critique is closed; remaining work is metadata patch plus optional polish.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 6 review of WIN-061 triggered by priority-queue item 246 (pushed 2026-04-23T14:29:16.067Z by decider after c5 self-apply patches: tldr_evidence rewrite (H1), PRED-SR-SUPPRESS / PRED-093 timestamp sentences (H2 / ISS-1418), SPE/CME mechanism direction (H3), W027-PRED-R006 cross-doc framing (H4), lowest plane-parallel axial-mode wording (H5)). The queue reason field references H3, H4, H5 as outstanding and asks for re-review for thoroughness. On full re-read of the c6 text, all five c5 holes (H1-H5) are resolved — the queue note slightly understates the c5 patch coverage.

STATUS OF C5 HOLES AFTER C6 PATCH BATCH:
- C5 H1 (major, TLDR Pazos/SPE conflation): RESOLVED. tldr_evidence now reads Pazos-primary + S&F-supporting-SPE (cavity-Q enhancement) + other studies with mixed-direction framing. Length 446 -> 557 chars; structure matches c5 recommendation verbatim.
- C5 H2 (major, ISS-1418 timestamp-forward): RESOLVED. V51.1 Update paragraph now contains the two recommended sentences plus the cryptographic-hash content-integrity vs temporal-priority sentence. Wording matches c5 recommendation closely.
- C5 H3 (moderate, SPE/CME mechanism direction): RESOLVED. Parenthetical rewritten with SPE protons (10-100 MeV) penetrating deeper into D-region and CME-driven auroral electrons (1-10 keV) primarily depositing in E-region/thermosphere. This is the canonical-correct direction.
- C5 H4 (moderate, cross-doc W027-PRED-R006): RESOLVED. predictions.json W027-PRED-R006 detail_reasoning now reads 'as WIN-061 documents, the storm-SR literature is split on direction' rather than asserting standard physics uniformly predicts suppression.
- C5 H5 (moderate, 'lowest possible' heuristic): RESOLVED. Now reads 'lowest plane-parallel axial-mode frequency from this H(r), ~8.8 Hz — a first-order lower bound — still above observed.' Matches c5 recommendation.

NEW THIS CYCLE:

(1) C6 H1 (moderate). predictions.json PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 structured fields contradict their own detail_reasoning prose. Both have data_predates_registration:false, is_genuinely_prospective:true, our_verdict:pending. The detail_reasoning prose for both records argues data WAS available before registration and prospectivity is hollow. The W027-PRED-R006 cross-doc inconsistency was fixed in c5 (closed in c6), but a generalization of the same problem remains within these two records. This is the only above-minor finding for c6.

(2) C6 H2 (minor, carry-forward). c5 stronger_arguments item 1 — Timestamp Error tab cross-reference — still not wired. The cryptographic-hash sentence is the perfect anchor.

(3) C6 H3 (minor, carry-forward). c5 stronger_arguments item 2 — WIN-038 pole-only floor cross-reference — still not wired in WIN-061 paragraph 1.

(4) C6 H4 (minor, carry-forward). c5 stronger_arguments item 3 — WIN-034 copper firmament mutual-exclusion — still not wired in V51.1 Update paragraph.

(5) C6 H5 (minor). 'behaviour' British spelling persists — deferred from c5.

ON THE OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (user-preference applied):

The c5-to-c6 decider patch batch is excellent. Every c5 recommendation lands as suggested or closely. The SPE/CME mechanism direction in particular was the highest-domain-expertise correction in c5 and the new wording is precise. The PRED-093 cryptographic-hash content-integrity vs temporal-priority distinction in the V51.1 Update paragraph is a meaningfully novel sentence — it doesn't just close ISS-1418, it opens the door to the canary-pattern cross-reference (which remains unwired). The W027-PRED-R006 cross-doc fix is clean.

The c6 H1 finding (predictions.json structured fields vs prose) is a fair critique: a defender doing a structured query would see is_genuinely_prospective:true and conclude the predictions are clean. But it could also be read sympathetically: the field semantics are about the FORMAL prospective status (was the prediction registered before the test event for which the dome's monitoring criterion is defined?), not about whether contextual background data was available. Under that semantic reading, the fields are technically correct and our prose adds the post-hoc concern as a separate argument. This sympathetic reading is the basis for the moderate (not major) severity rating: the inconsistency is exploitable but not slam-dunk wrong. The fix is either field-semantic clarification or adding a companion field; either is a metadata patch, not a verdict-changing fix.

The three carry-forward minor items (Timestamp Error tab, WIN-038, WIN-034 cross-references) are not verdict-threatening — they are unrealized-strengthening opportunities. The Self-Contradicted verdict stands without them. Surfacing them in a minor ranking is consistent with c5's framing: at this point the WIN is clean enough that further work is polish-and-cross-reference rather than verdict-defense.

A fair dome-defender reading c6 would concede that all my c5 critiques landed and the prose is now in good shape. The defender's three remaining attacks (the metadata inconsistency, the unwired Timestamp Error reference, the unwired WIN-038/WIN-034 cross-references) are all real but only the first is even moderately leveraged. The metadata inconsistency is the only one that could plausibly affect a third-party assessment of our review's internal consistency.

Verdict confidence rises from c5 (0.94) to c6 (0.96). The verdict is safe.

2. [minor] Decider self-apply: parenthetical to cryptographic-hash sentence cross-referencing Timestamp Error tab. (closes c6 H2)
3. [minor] Decider self-apply: one sentence cross-referencing WIN-038 pole-only floor. (closes c6 H3)
4. [minor] Decider self-apply: one sentence cross-referencing WIN-034 copper firmament mutual-exclusion. (closes c6 H4)
5. [minor] Spelling polish 'behaviour' -> 'behavior'. (closes c6 H5)

All five items are independent. Items 2-5 are short-string EXPs or single-sentence decider self-applies; item 1 is a separate predictions.json metadata patch. The verdict needs no defending; remaining work is polish.
